title: Why Innodata (INOD) Is Up 92.8% After Raising 2026 Outlook On Record Q1 And Big Tech Deal
text: Innodata Inc. experienced significant stock appreciation following strong first-quarter 2026 results and an upgraded financial outlook. The company reported sales rising to US$90.1 million and net income to US$14.9 million, marking record performance. Management subsequently raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to approximately 40% or higher growth. A major catalyst for this optimism is a newly announced engagement worth $51 million with a significant technology company, anticipated to become one of Innodata's largest client relationships. The investment thesis centers on whether demand for high-quality AI data services can sustain both rapid expansion and solid profit margins despite increasing automation trends. However, a primary concern persists: customer concentration risk. The new Big Tech contract, while substantial, actually intensifies this vulnerability rather than alleviating it, as it adds to reliance on major clients. The narrative projects 2029 revenue reaching $549.1 million with earnings of $73.5 million, requiring sustained annual revenue growth of approximately 30%. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests a fair valuation of $91.25 per share, implying modest upside potential.
